steps.title: Find the main lengths
steps.content: Point $P$ is the midpoint of segment $NQ$, and $PQ=52$. Therefore, $NP=52$ and $NQ=104$.

steps.title: Compute both areas
steps.content:
Since segment $NP$ is the diameter of the semicircle, its radius is $26$. The rectangle has dimensions $104$ by $18$.
$$A_{\text{semicircle}}=\frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2=338\pi$$
$$A_{\text{rectangle}}=104\cdot18=1{,}872$$

steps.title: Compare to the given form
steps.content:
The total area is
$$338\pi+1{,}872$$
So $a=338$ and $b=1{,}872$. Then
$$a-b=338-1{,}872=-1{,}534$$
The answer is -1534.
